title: Praxis School Counselor (5422) — 200-Item Diagnostic Bank
description: Two independently authored 100-question diagnostic batches linked to a 12-chapter learning library, targeted skill practice, and an optional 120-question timed simulation. The official 5422 test currently has 120 selected-response questions in 120 minutes; AlloFlow practice is not an official form or score.
contentReview: 200 source-reviewed questions plus 12 chapters, 60 checks, 75 flashcards, and 20 memory aids; independent school-counselor and psychometric review pending
disclaimer: Independent preparation material. Not affiliated with or endorsed by ETS or ASCA. The official Praxis School Counselor (5422) test currently has 120 selected-response questions in 120 minutes; the 100-item diagnostic batches are not official-length forms. Practice results are not official or scaled Praxis scores, pass predictions, credentials, mental-health treatment, legal advice, or substitutes for current federal, state, local, district, crisis, mandated-reporting, and ethical requirements.

Hide the result to protect program credibility.
Select a different outcome after seeing the data and call it the original goal.
Blame students or families for the shortfall.
items.rationale: Transparent reporting builds trust and learning. A missed goal can guide improvement when evidence, limitations, and next decisions are communicated without blame.
choiceRationales: Correct. Transparent reporting builds trust and learning. A missed goal can guide improvement when evidence, limitations, and next decisions are communicated without blame.

items.prompt: A counselor is advocating for staffing. What should the proposal include?
choices: A national ratio quoted without local context.
Local needs, service-access and time-use data, outcome gaps, program capacity, credible research, a defined staffing plan, and measures for evaluating impact.
A statement that counselors feel busy.
Confidential anecdotes identifying vulnerable students.
items.rationale: Data-based advocacy integrates local need, capacity, equity, research, feasible resources, privacy, and an evaluation plan.

items.prompt: A systemic change improves equitable enrollment after one semester. Which follow-up is best?
choices: Assume the improvement will persist without monitoring.
Return immediately to the former procedure.
Monitor durability and unintended effects, gather stakeholder feedback, refine procedures, and consider responsible expansion.
Expand districtwide before checking implementation quality.
items.rationale: Improvement requires follow-up for maintenance, equity, implementation, and unintended consequences before scaling. This choice best integrates the student need, professional role, available evidence, and ethical or program principle described in the scenario.

items.prompt: Several needs remain after the program review. Which prioritization process is most defensible?
choices: Select the easiest activity regardless of need.
Use counselor preference as the only criterion.
Choose the concern with the highest raw count without examining rates or impact.
Integrate outcome gaps, student and family voice, urgency and safety, equity, feasibility, evidence, and alignment with the program mission.
items.rationale: Program priorities require transparent judgment across need, voice, risk, equity, feasibility, evidence, alignment, and available capacity.
